scenario: 1 board, 3 cards (Y.Map / JSON), 2 clients in-process, 30 card-move iterations
perf_baseline_device_note: Target = Galaxy Tab S6 Lite, Chrome Android. WSL2 cannot host a real tablet run; numbers below are localhost upper bounds for correctness + payload size. Tablet parse/eval cost is estimated from bundle size (§bundles).

runs.setup: yjs 13.6.30 + y-websocket 3.0.0 client + inline WSS server on port 3201
runs.notes: Binary CRDT wire format. 58 B is a single Y.Map set encoded as one Item. Matches docs.yjs.dev claim that overhead is op-count-bound.

runs.setup: socket.io 4.8.3 server + socket.io-client on port 3202; JSON events
runs.notes: JSON(event) size 82 B. Raw ws + JSON would drop cold-start to ~5 ms but add dev work for rooms/reconnect.

Real echo test requires vendor account + network egress. SDK surface verified (createClient, enterRoom etc.). Payload & latency cited from vendor docs + community. Follow-up: live test with Pro sandbox key before merge.

tablet_perf_extrapolation.method: bundle cost × Tab S6 Lite parse rate (Chrome Android, Helio P22T ≈ 1.5-2 MB/s JS parse)
tablet_perf_extrapolation.disclaimer: Extrapolated only. Real tablet measurement required in a follow-up feature task before production rollout.
